feature_name: AEP Library — Custom Adversary Emulation Pathway Builder
workflow_stages: Publish and make available for campaigns

competitors.name: KnowBe4
competitors.setup_friction: None beyond admin login — template creation is immediately accessible
core_flow: Navigate to Phishing Templates
Browse System Templates (managed, read-only) or My Templates (custom)
Clone a system template OR create new via WYSIWYG editor OR use AIDA AI generator
AIDA path: write plain-language description, select attack vector type, set difficulty 1–5 stars
AI generates template; admin reviews and edits
Save to My Templates
Preview renders email as recipient would see it
Optional: send test email to self before campaign
competitors.error_prevention: Difficulty slider with NIST Phish Scale SEI mapping prevents admins from accidentally creating templates that are too obvious or too deceptive
competitors.trust_model: Managed vs. custom separation is clear; clone path ensures custom work starts from a validated baseline
competitors.rbac_notes: Admin role required; all admins have equal template authoring access; no per-template ownership or reviewer role
competitors.repeat_use_efficiency: Clone is fast; AIDA Quick Create mode reduces visible fields; saved templates are reusable across campaigns
competitors.ai_personalization: AIDA generates templates from plain-language description + structured parameters; AI selects social engineering indicators based on difficulty level
competitors.remediation_path: Not connected to template authoring — separate training assignment flow
competitors.source_notes: Based on public KnowBe4 Knowledge Base docs: template creation guide, AIDA guide, advanced features guide — authoritative for admin UX

competitors.name: Adaptive Security
competitors.entry_point: Admin platform → Personas section (exact nav inferred from blog)
competitors.setup_friction: Executive deepfake requires executive name/role to be registered; OSINT-driven personas require no setup
core_flow: Admin selects an executive to impersonate (for deepfake persona) or selects OSINT auto-generation
Platform generates AI voice clone and video avatar from available data
Admin does not write a behavior prompt — persona is derived from the real person being impersonated
Custom persona is available for use in vishing/video simulations
feedback_states: Preview of generated voice/video (inferred from deepfake demo product)
competitors.trust_model: Deepfake generation from real executives implies strong consent and governance — not publicly documented
competitors.ai_personalization: Core product — AI generates all attack content including OSINT-personalized emails and deepfake video/voice
competitors.risk_score_surface: Not observed in persona builder context
competitors.source_notes: Based on product blog post about Custom AI Deepfake Personas and marketing landing pages — admin UX internals are inferred, not directly observed

competitors.name: Hoxhunt
competitors.setup_friction: No custom scenario authoring — admin only configures which Hoxhunt-managed scenarios are active
core_flow: Admin enables/disables threat types (email, Slack, Teams, SMS, deepfake video)
Admin sets difficulty range per group
Platform adaptively delivers scenarios based on individual employee performance history
Admin cannot author new scenarios or personas
feedback_states: Real-time dashboard updates as simulations are delivered and reported
Per-user difficulty trajectory visible in reporting
competitors.error_prevention: No authoring = no authoring errors; admin controls are configuration, not creation
competitors.trust_model: All scenarios are Hoxhunt-managed and validated — admin trust is implicit in the platform's content curation
competitors.rbac_notes: Admin role controls simulation settings; employees see only their own training
competitors.repeat_use_efficiency: Fully automated after setup — no per-campaign template selection required
competitors.ai_personalization: AI adapts difficulty per employee; scenarios are personalized by language, role, department
competitors.risk_score_surface: Behavioral signals tracked; not a named risk score in the same way as Dune
competitors.source_notes: Based on public product pages, help docs, and blog articles — admin console access not directly observed

competitors.name: Breacher.ai
competitors.setup_friction: Playbook mode requires minimal setup; custom campaign mode requires selecting attack chain steps
core_flow: Admin selects a ready-made playbook (e.g. BEC Wire Transfer, IT Help Desk Impersonation) OR builds a custom attack chain
Custom chain: selects attack types to sequence (email + voice + deepfake video) and configures targeting
AI generates conversation content at runtime — admin does not author behavioral prompts
Campaign is deployed; AI agents conduct conversations adaptively
competitors.trust_model: Playbooks are pre-validated by Breacher; custom campaigns rely on admin judgment for attack chain configuration
competitors.repeat_use_efficiency: Playbooks are reusable; custom chains can likely be saved as templates (inferred)
competitors.ai_personalization: AI generates adaptive conversation content at runtime — not admin-authored
competitors.source_notes: Based on product marketing pages and press release — admin campaign builder UX not directly documented

observed_patterns: Clone-and-customize is the primary path from managed library to custom artifact (KnowBe4)
Guided AI generation (plain-language brief + structured parameters) is more admin-friendly than raw prompt entry (KnowBe4 AIDA)
Difficulty as a slider or star rating abstracts behavioral complexity into an admin-legible control (KnowBe4 AIDA)
Visual and navigational separation between managed (vendor) and custom (tenant) artifacts is essential (KnowBe4)
Attack categorization by channel is the market default — psychological technique taxonomy is an open differentiator (all competitors)
No competitor exposes a raw system prompt editor as the default authoring interface
No competitor offers a live conversational simulator for testing AI agent behavior before deployment
anti_patterns: Raw system prompt box as default authoring interface — too high cognitive load for non-technical admins; produces low-quality artifacts
No behavioral preview before deployment — all competitors have this gap; do not repeat it
Attack type classified by channel only — limits analytics to delivery mechanism, not cognitive vulnerability
No version-locking for deployed templates — safe for static email artifacts but dangerous for live conversational AI agents
Template name collisions in large libraries — enforce unique names and display metadata (method, created date) on cards
differentiation_opportunities: Live conversational simulator — genuine market gap; no competitor offers admin-interactive AEP behavior testing
Adversary Method as psychological technique taxonomy (Authority, Urgency, Reciprocity, Curiosity, Scarcity, Familiarity) — enables risk reporting on cognitive vulnerability, not just channel
AI-assisted AEP generation from plain-language scenario brief — makes builder accessible to non-technical admins
Outcome criteria tied to risk scoring — connects AEP definition to downstream employee risk score calculation (unique to Dune)
Dune-seeded AEPs as quality benchmarks and teaching templates — clone-and-customize from expert-authored examples
design_implications: Default builder flow: plain-language scenario brief + Adversary Method select + behavior parameters → AI generates starting prompt and workflow → admin refines
Advanced mode: expose raw system prompt for technical admins who want direct control
Duplicate from Dune-seeded AEP should be a first-class CTA on seeded cards, not a secondary option
Library grid must visually distinguish Dune-seeded (read-only, reference quality) from Custom (editable, admin-owned) with more than a label
Simulator must be a visible progress step in the builder flow, not optional — AEP card should show simulator test status
Adversary Method must be a controlled vocabulary select populated before design begins — not free-form text
confidence_notes: No competitor was directly observed operating a conversational AI agent persona builder — market gap claim is well-supported
KnowBe4 AIDA observations are based on public KB docs — high confidence for admin workflow details
Adaptive Security persona builder internals are inferred from product blog and marketing — medium confidence
Breacher.ai admin UX is not publicly documented — observations are based on marketing pages only
